nativeRange.habitat: Cultivar of Bucephalandra pygmaea, named after Bukit Kelam (a granite inselberg in West Kalimantan, Borneo). Like other Bucephalandra, occurs as a rheophyte on rocks and submerged wood along fast-flowing rainforest streams in Borneo, periodically emersed and resubmerged with seasonal water-level changes.

decorPreferences: lava rock, driftwood, or porous hardscape to attach the rhizome to
moderate flow across the rhizome to mimic Borneo rheophyte streams
low to moderate lighting; tolerates shaded foreground or midground placement
stable, established tank — avoid newly cycled systems

goodWith: Neocaridina and Caridina shrimp which graze biofilm without harming the plant
other low-light epiphytes such as Anubias, Java fern and mosses
avoidWith: large herbivorous or rhizome-disturbing fish such as goldfish, silver dollars and large plecos
vigorous diggers (large cichlids) that may dislodge attached rhizomes
newly set up tanks with unstable parameters

summary: A compact Bucephalandra pygmaea cultivar named after Bukit Kelam mountain in Borneo, featuring small wavy-edged dark to bright green leaves often speckled with tiny white dots. Slow-growing epiphyte for low-light foreground or midground hardscape.
careNotes: Bucephalandra 'Bukit Kelam' is a small cultivar of Bucephalandra pygmaea, named after Bukit Kelam — a prominent granite inselberg in West Kalimantan, Indonesian Borneo, near where the parent species was collected. Husbandry is the same as the genus (see parent entry fw-plant-016): the creeping rhizome must be attached to lava rock, driftwood, slate or other hardscape using super glue gel or thread, and must not be buried in substrate or it will rot. Both KeepFishKeeping and Aquarium Co-Op describe it as slow-growing with compact leaves roughly 2 cm wide × 5 cm long (KFK) or about 5-10 cm total height with ~5 cm × 2 cm leaves (Aquarium Co-Op); these are consistent. Leaves are wavy-edged and develop characteristic small white spots/speckles on the leaf surface, especially when grown emersed. Lower-light placement is generally advised (KFK), though medium light commonly brings out richer color and any iridescent sheen typical of Bucephalandra. CO2 is not required but is generally reported to improve growth rate and coloration. Tissue-cultured stock arrives clean of algae, snails and pest hitchhikers and is the preferred source format. Like other Bucephalandra, freshly imported plants commonly experience 'buce melt' — older emersed-grown leaves dropping when transitioned to submersed conditions — but recover from the rhizome over several weeks if introduced into a stable, established tank. Branches readily from the rhizome even without trimming, simplifying propagation. Detailed water parameters are not stated on either product page; values shown here are inherited from the well-documented genus-level husbandry (approximately 22-28 °C, pH ~6.0-7.5, gH 3-15) and apply to this cultivar.
breedingNotes: Propagation is by vegetative rhizome division, as with all Bucephalandra. KeepFishKeeping notes 'Bukit Kelam' branches readily on its own without active trimming. Once a side branch has its own leaves and roots, cut the rhizome at a natural bend with clean scissors and attach the new division to a separate piece of hardscape.

primary.name: KeepFishKeeping
primary.notes: Primary source. Confirmed scientific name Bucephalandra pygmaea 'Bukit Kelam', tissue-cultured form, Southeast Asia rheophyte origin, leaf dimensions ~2 cm × 5 cm, slow-growing, low light preference, epiphyte (rhizome must not be buried), easy care, branches readily for propagation.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name (Bucephalandra pygmaea 'Bukit Kelam'), adult height ~5-10 cm (2-4 inches), slow growth, wavy-edged bright green leaves with small white-dot speckling, tissue-cultured format pest/algae free. Product page does not list quantitative water parameters.

additional.name: AquaMate genus entry (Bucephalandra)
additional.notes: Inherited husbandry parameters (temperature 22-28 °C, pH 6.0-7.5, gH 3-15, lighting/CO2/fertilization profile, rhizome attachment, buce-melt acclimation behavior) from the previously researched parent entry fw-plant-016, which cross-references Tropica, Aquarium Co-Op (genus guide), 2HR Aquarist, Aquariadise and AquariumBreeder. Both product pages for this cultivar are silent on numeric water parameters, so the genus values are applied to this cultivar by inheritance.
